name: intentional-pipecat

metadata.name: intentional-pipecat
metadata.summary: Plugin that lets you build transcribed audio voice bots using Pipecat

documentation:
# Intentional - Pipecat

[![Made for Intentional](https://img.shields.io/badge/made_for-intentional-blue)](https://intentional-ai.github.io/intentional/docs/home/)
[![PyPI - Version](https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/intentional-pipecat.svg)](https://pypi.org/project/intentional-pipecat)
[![PyPI - Python Version](https://img.shields.io/pypi/pyversions/intentional-pipecat.svg)](https://pypi.org/project/intentional-pipecat)
[![PyPI - Downloads](https://img.shields.io/pypi/dm/intentional-pipecat)](https://pypistats.org/packages/intentional-pipecat)
[![Copier](https://img.shields.io/endpoint?url=https://raw.githubusercontent.com/copier-org/copier/master/img/badge/badge-grayscale-inverted-border-orange.json)](https://github.com/copier-org/copier)

Plugin that lets you build transcribed audio voice bots using Pipecat.

-----

## Table of Contents

- [Installation](#installation)
- [License](#license)

## Installation

You can install `intentional-pipecat` without extras:

```console
pip install intentional-pipecat[silero,deepgram,openai]
```

However, it will be unusable as-is, because it will come with nearly no dependencies.

In order to have any of the underlying dependencies installed (such as Silero's VAD, Deepgram, OpenAI, etc...) you must specify them as extras, like:

```console
pip install intentional-pipecat[silero,deepgram,openai]
```

For a list of all the available extras, see Pipecat's documentation or [their `pyproject.toml` file](https://github.com/pipecat-ai/pipecat/blob/main/pyproject.toml). We are going to try to keep this list up-to-date on Intentional's end, but in case of issues you can also install those dependencies by hand by doing:

```console
pip install pipecat-ai[<the extras you want>]
```

This guarantees that you get the correct extras for your version of Pipecat. Please open an issue if you find any discrepancies.

## Usage

In order to use Pipecat, you need to specify which components you want to use in the configuration file. For example:

```yaml
bot:
  type: pipecat
  llm:
    client: openai
    name: gpt-4o
  vad:
    module: silero
    client: SileroVADAnalyzer
  stt:
    module: deepgram
    client: DeepgramSTTService
  tts:
    module: azure
    client: AzureTTSService
```

This example would require the extras `silero`, `deepgram` and `azure`:

```console
pip install intentional-pipecat[silero,deepgram,azure]
```

See [Pipecat's documentation](https://docs.pipecat.ai/getting-started/overview) for more information about what modules and classes are available for the various pipeline components.

At this time, the Pipeline structure itself cannot be configured. [Open an issue](https://github.com/intentional-ai/intentional/issues/new) if you'd like to allow some degree of pipeline customization.

## License

`intentional` is distributed under the terms of the [AGPL](LICENSE.txt) license. If that doesn't work for you, [get in touch](mailto:github@zansara.dev).

classes.name: intentional_pipecat.frame_processor.UserToLLMFrameProcessor

methods.name: intentional_pipecat.frame_processor.UserToLLMFrameProcessor.process_frame
documentation: Processes the incoming frames if relevant.
FrameProcessor that takes the user input and sends it to the LLM.

classes.name: intentional_pipecat.frame_processor.LLMToUserFrameProcessor

methods.name: intentional_pipecat.frame_processor.LLMToUserFrameProcessor.process_frame
documentation: Simply forwards all framews ahead. The default behavior of FrameProcessor is to block them instead.

FrameProcessor that takes the LLM output and sends it to the user.

Note: this processor itself is doing nothing else than changing the default behavior of `process_frame()` to not
swallow frames when they reach it. The processor is actually used by `PipecatBotStructure` to generate frames when
a reply from the LLM is received.

modules.name: intentional_pipecat.transport
documentation: Pipecat transport class implementation that is compatible with Intentional.
SPDX-FileCopyrightText: 2024-present ZanSara <github@zansara.dev>
SPDX-License-Identifier: AGPL-3.0-or-later

classes.name: intentional_pipecat.transport.AudioInputTransport

methods.name: intentional_pipecat.transport.AudioInputTransport.send_audio_frame
documentation: Public method used by the Intentional bot structure to publish audio of user's speech to the Pipecat pipeline.

methods.name: intentional_pipecat.transport.AudioInputTransport.start
documentation: Starts the transport's resources.

methods.name: intentional_pipecat.transport.AudioInputTransport.cleanup
documentation: Cleans up the transport's resources.

Pipecat input transport class implementation that is compatible with Intentional (supports audio only).

This class' task is to take the user's input and convert it into frames that Pipecat can process.

classes.name: intentional_pipecat.transport.AudioOutputTransport

methods.name: intentional_pipecat.transport.AudioOutputTransport.start
documentation: Starts the transport's resources.

methods.name: intentional_pipecat.transport.AudioOutputTransport.cleanup
documentation: Cleans up the transport's resources.

methods.name: intentional_pipecat.transport.AudioOutputTransport.process_frame
documentation:
When it receives a TTSAudioRawFrame, makes the llm emit a `on_audio_message_from_llm` event with the content
of the frame.

methods.name: intentional_pipecat.transport.AudioOutputTransport._audio_out_task_handler
documentation: Internal: overrides the method of the base class to not perform a few actions we don't need.

Pipecat output transport class implementation that is compatible with Intentional (supports audio only).

This class' task is to take the audio frames generated by the TTS and publish them through events that Intentional
can understand.

classes.name: intentional_pipecat.transport.AudioTransport
documentation:
Pipecat transport class implementation that is compatible with Intentional (supports audio only).

This class is a simple wrapper around AudioInputTransport and AudioOutputTransport, that makes sure both classes
receive the same parameters at initialization.

documentation: Pipecat bot structure implementation.
SPDX-FileCopyrightText: 2024-present ZanSara <github@zansara.dev>

classes.name: intentional_pipecat.bot_structure.PipecatBotStructure

methods.name: intentional_pipecat.bot_structure.PipecatBotStructure.__init__
documentation:
Args:
    config:
        The configuration dictionary for the bot structure.
        It includes only the LLM definition under the `llm` key.

methods.name: intentional_pipecat.bot_structure.PipecatBotStructure.connect
documentation: Initializes the model and connects to it as/if necessary.

methods.name: intentional_pipecat.bot_structure.PipecatBotStructure.handle_llm_text_messages
documentation: Sends the text message to the Pipecat pipeline to be converted into audio.

methods.name: intentional_pipecat.bot_structure.PipecatBotStructure.handle_llm_starts_generating_response
documentation: Warns the Pipecat pipeline of the start of a response from the LLM by sending an LLMFullResponseStartFrame()

methods.name: intentional_pipecat.bot_structure.PipecatBotStructure.handle_llm_stops_generating_response
documentation: Warns the Pipecat pipeline of the end of a response from the LLM by sending an LLMFullResponseEndFrame()

methods.name: intentional_pipecat.bot_structure.PipecatBotStructure.disconnect
documentation: Disconnects from the LLM and unloads/closes it as/if necessary.

methods.name: intentional_pipecat.bot_structure.PipecatBotStructure.run
documentation: Main loop for the bot.

methods.name: intentional_pipecat.bot_structure.PipecatBotStructure.send
intentional_pipecat.bot_structure.PipecatBotStructure.handle_interruption
documentation:
Handle an interruption in the streaming.

Args:
    lenght_to_interruption: The length of the data that was produced to the user before the interruption.
        This value could be number of characters, number of words, milliseconds, number of audio frames, etc.
        depending on the bot structure that implements it.
Bot structure that uses Pipecat to make text-only models able to handle spoken input.
